cigar.definition: Tobacco rolled and wrapped with an outer covering of tobacco leaves, intended to be smoked.

rebut.definition: To drive back or beat back; to repulse.

humph.definition: To utter "humph!" in doubt or disapproval.

blush.definition: An act of blushing; a red glow on the face caused by shame, modesty, etc.

focal.definition: Belonging to, concerning, or located at a focus

evade.definition: To get away from by cunning; to avoid by dexterity, subterfuge, address, or ingenuity; to elude; to cleverly escape from

naval.definition: Of or relating to a navy.

serve.definition: An act of putting the ball or shuttlecock in play in various games.

heath.definition: A tract of level uncultivated land with sandy soil and scrubby vegetation; heathland.

dwarf.definition: Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.

model.definition: A person who serves as a subject for artwork or fashion, usually in the medium of photography but also for painting or drawing.

karma.definition: The sum total of a person's actions, which determine the person's next incarnation in samsara, the cycle of death and rebirth.

bench.definition: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.

feign.definition: To make a false show or pretence of; to counterfeit or simulate.

major.definition: A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.

death.definition: The cessation of life and all associated processes; the end of an organism's existence as an entity independent from its environment and its return to an inert, nonliving state.

fresh.definition: A rush of water, along a river or onto the land; a flood.

crust.definition: A more solid, dense or hard layer on a surface or boundary.

stool.definition: A seat, especially for one person and without armrests.

abase.definition: To lower, as in condition in life, office, rank, etc., so as to cause pain or hurt feelings; to degrade, to depress, to humble, to humiliate.

marry.definition: To enter into the conjugal or connubial state; to take a husband or a wife.

react.definition: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

pride.definition: The quality or state of being proud; an unreasonable overestimation of one's own superiority in terms of talents, looks, wealth, importance etc., which manifests itself in lofty airs, distance, reserve and often contempt of others.

floss.definition: A thread used to clean the gaps between the teeth.

helix.definition: A curve on the surface of a cylinder or cone such that its angle to a plane perpendicular to the axis is constant; the three-dimensional curve seen in a screw or a spiral staircase.

croak.definition: A faint, harsh sound made in the throat.

staff.definition: (plural staffs or staves) A long, straight, thick wooden rod or stick, especially one used to assist in walking.

paper.definition: A sheet material used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.

unfed.definition: A mosquito that has not had a blood meal.

whelp.definition: A young offspring of a canid (ursid, felid, pinniped), especially of a dog or a wolf, the young of a bear or similar mammal (lion, tiger, seal); a pup, wolf cub.

trawl.definition: A net or dragnet used for trawling.

outdo.definition: To excel; go beyond in performance; surpass.

adobe.definition: An unburnt brick dried in the sun.

crazy.definition: An insane or eccentric person; a crackpot.

digit.definition: A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university or, in some countries, a college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)

crate.definition: A large open box or basket, used especially to transport fragile goods.

cluck.definition: The sound made by a hen, especially when brooding, or calling her chicks.

spike.definition: In a violin-family instrument, the carved wooden plug which sits in the bottom block of the instrument.

mimic.definition: A person who practices mimicry, or mime.

pound.definition: A unit of mass equal to 16 avoirdupois ounces (= 453.592 37 g). Today this value is the most common meaning of "pound" as a unit of weight.

maxim.definition: A self-evident axiom or premise; a pithy expression of a general principle or rule.

linen.definition: Thread or cloth made from flax fiber.

flesh.definition: The soft tissue of the body, especially muscle and fat.

forth.definition: Forward in time, place or degree.

first.definition: The person or thing in the first position.

belly.definition: The abdomen, especially a fat one.

ivory.definition: The hard white form of dentin which forms the tusks of elephants, walruses and other animals.

print.definition: Books and other material created by printing presses, considered collectively or as a medium.

yearn.definition: To long, have a strong desire (for something).

drain.definition: A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)

bribe.definition: Something (usually money) given in exchange for influence or as an inducement to dishonesty.

stout.definition: A dark and strong malt brew made with toasted grain.

panel.definition: A (usually) rectangular section of a surface, or of a covering or of a wall, fence etc.

crass.definition: Coarse; crude; unrefined or insensitive; lacking discrimination

flume.definition: A ravine or gorge, usually one with water running through.

offal.definition: The internal organs of an animal, used as animal food.

agree.definition: To harmonize in opinion, statement, or action; to be in unison or concord; to be or become united or consistent; to concur.

error.definition: The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.

argue.definition: To show grounds for concluding (that); to indicate, imply.

bleed.definition: An incident of bleeding, as in haemophilia.

delta.definition: The fourth letter of the modern Greek alphabet Δ, δ.

flick.definition: A short, quick movement, especially a brush, sweep, or flip.

totem.definition: Any natural object or living creature that serves as an emblem of a tribe, clan or family.

front.definition: The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.

shrub.definition: A woody plant smaller than a tree, and usually with several stems from the same base.

parry.definition: A defensive or deflective action; an act of parrying.

biome.definition: Any major regional biological community such as that of forest or desert

lapel.definition: Each of the two triangular pieces of cloth on the front of a jacket or coat that are folded back below the throat, leaving a triangular opening between.

greet.definition: To welcome in a friendly manner, either in person or through another means e.g. writing or over the phone/internet

goner.definition: Someone (or something) doomed; a hopeless case.

golem.definition: A humanoid creature made from clay, animated by magic.

lusty.definition: Exhibiting lust (in the obsolete sense meaning "vigor"); strong, healthy, robust; vigorous; full of sap or vitality.

round.definition: So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.

lying.definition: To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.

gamma.definition: The third letter of the Greek alphabet (Γ, γ), preceded by beta (Β, β) and followed by delta, (Δ, δ).

labor.definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.

civic.definition: Of, relating to, or belonging to a city, a citizen, or citizenship; municipal or civil.

forge.definition: Furnace or hearth where metals are heated prior to hammering them into shape.

moult.definition: The process of shedding or losing a covering of fur, feathers or skin etc.

basic.definition: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.

salad.definition: A food made primarily of a mixture of raw or cold ingredients, typically vegetables, usually served with a dressing such as vinegar or mayonnaise.

agate.definition: A semi-pellucid, uncrystallized variety of quartz, presenting various tints in the same specimen, with colors delicately arranged in stripes or bands, or blended in clouds.

spicy.definition: Of, pertaining to, or containing spice.

spray.definition: A fine, gentle, dispersed mist of liquid.

essay.definition: (authorship) A written composition of moderate length, exploring a particular issue or subject.

fjord.definition: A long, narrow, deep inlet between cliffs.

spend.definition: Amount of money spent (during a period); expenditure.

kebab.definition: A dish of pieces of meat, fish, or vegetables roasted on a skewer or spit, especially a doner kebab.

guild.definition: A group or association mainly of tradespeople made up of merchants, craftspeople, or artisans for mutual aid, particularly in the Middle Ages.

aback.definition: Towards the back or rear; backwards.

motor.definition: A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.

hatch.definition: A horizontal door in a floor or ceiling.

thumb.definition: The short thick digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.

dowry.definition: Payment, such as property or money, paid by the bride's family to the groom or his family at the time of marriage.

belch.definition: The sound one makes when belching.

pilot.definition: A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.

tweed.definition: A coarse woolen fabric used for clothing.

comet.definition: A celestial body consisting mainly of ice, dust and gas in a (usually very eccentric) orbit around the Sun and having a "tail" of matter blown back from it by the solar wind as it approaches the Sun.

enema.definition: An injection of fluid into the large intestine by way of the rectum, usually for medical purposes.

steed.definition: A stallion, especially in the sense of mount.

abyss.definition: Hell; the bottomless pit; primeval chaos; a confined subterranean ocean.

growl.definition: A deep, rumbling, threatening sound made in the throat by an animal.

fling.definition: An act of throwing, often violently.

world.definition: (with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.

briar.definition: Any of many plants with thorny stems growing in dense clusters, such as many in the Rosa, Rubus, and Smilax genera.

great.definition: A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.

altar.definition: A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.

coast.definition: The edge of the land where it meets an ocean, sea, gulf, bay, or large lake.

rogue.definition: A scoundrel, rascal or unprincipled, deceitful, and unreliable person.

gaudy.definition: One of the large beads in the rosary at which the paternoster is recited.

chill.definition: A moderate, but uncomfortable and penetrating coldness.

vodka.definition: A clear distilled alcoholic liquor made from grain mash.

surer.definition: Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.

radio.definition: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.

rouge.definition: Red or pink makeup to add colour to the cheeks; blusher.

perch.definition: Any of the three species of spiny-finned freshwater fish in the genus Perca.

wrote.definition: To form letters, words or symbols on a surface in order to communicate.

clock.definition: An instrument used to measure or keep track of time; a non-portable timepiece.

store.definition: A place where items may be accumulated or routinely kept.

solve.definition: (chiefly law enforcement) A solution; an explanation.

cheat.definition: To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.

usher.definition: A person, in a church, cinema etc., who escorts people to their seats.

epoch.definition: A particular period of history, especially one considered noteworthy or remarkable.

vital.definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.

peach.definition: A tree (Prunus persica), native to China and now widely cultivated throughout temperate regions, having pink flowers and edible fruit.

champ.definition: Buddy, sport, mate (as a term of address)

baton.definition: A staff or truncheon, used for various purposes

gripe.definition: A complaint, often a petty or trivial one.

weary.definition: To make or to become weary.

acute.definition: A person who has the acute form of a disorder, such as schizophrenia.

ferry.definition: A ship used to transport people, smaller vehicles and goods from one port to another, usually on a regular schedule.

aside.definition: An incidental remark made quietly so as to be heard by the person to whom it is said and not by any others in the vicinity.

troll.definition: A supernatural being of varying size, now especially a grotesque humanoid creature living in caves or hills or under bridges.

slump.definition: A heavy or helpless collapse; a slouching or drooping posture; a period of poor activity or performance, especially an extended period.

panic.definition: Overpowering fright, often affecting groups of people or animals.

knoll.definition: A small mound or rounded hill.

sugar.definition: Sucrose in the form of small crystals, obtained from sugar cane or sugar beet and used to sweeten food and drink.

could.definition: (auxiliary verb, defective) To know how to; to be able to.

humor.definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.

cynic.definition: A person who believes that all people are motivated by selfishness.

aroma.definition: A smell; especially a pleasant spicy or fragrant one.

caulk.definition: A pointed projection on a horseshoe to prevent it slipping.

tacit.definition: Expressed in silence; implied, but not made explicit; silent.

trove.definition: A treasure trove; a collection of treasure.

bloke.definition: A man, a fellow; an ordinary man, a man on the street.

spill.definition: A mess of something that has been dropped.

chant.definition: Type of singing done generally without instruments and harmony.

choke.definition: A control on a carburetor to adjust the air/fuel mixture when the engine is cold.

rupee.definition: The common name for the monetary currencies used in modern India, Mauritius, Nepal, Pakistan, the Seychelles, or Sri Lanka, often abbreviated ₨.

cloth.definition: A woven fabric such as used in dressing, decorating, cleaning or other practical use.

sweet.definition: The basic taste sensation induced by sugar.

nymph.definition: (Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.

found.definition: To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.

shall.definition: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.

snout.definition: The long, projecting nose, mouth, and jaw of a beast, as of pigs.

comma.definition: The punctuation mark ⟨,⟩ used to indicate a set off parts of a sentence or between elements of a list.

stair.definition: A single step in a staircase.

black.definition: The colour/color perceived in the absence of light, but also when no light is reflected, but rather absorbed.

squad.definition: A group of people organized for some common purpose, usually of about ten members.

royal.definition: A royal person; a member of a royal family.

chunk.definition: A part of something that has been separated.

slave.definition: A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.

shame.definition: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.

cheek.definition: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.

ample.definition: Large; great in size, extent, capacity, or bulk; for example spacious, roomy or widely extended.

flair.definition: A natural or innate talent or aptitude.

foyer.definition: A lobby, corridor, or waiting room, used in a hotel, theater, etc.

cargo.definition: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.

oxide.definition: A binary chemical compound of oxygen with another chemical element.

plant.definition: An organism that is not an animal, especially an organism capable of photosynthesis. Typically a small or herbaceous organism of this kind, rather than a tree.

olive.definition: A tree, Olea europaea, cultivated since ancient times in the Mediterranean for its fruit and the oil obtained from it.

inert.definition: A substance that does not react chemically.

askew.definition: Turned or twisted to one side.

heist.definition: A robbery or burglary, especially from an institution such as a bank or museum.

shown.definition: To display, to have somebody see (something).

hasty.definition: Acting in haste; being too hurried or quick

trash.definition: Useless things to be discarded; rubbish; refuse.

fella.definition: (chiefly South US) used to address a male

story.definition: A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.

hairy.definition: Of a person, having a lot of hair on the body.

homer.definition: Various former units of volume, particularly:

midst.definition: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.

fetus.definition: An unborn or unhatched vertebrate showing signs of the mature animal.

butch.definition: A lesbian who appears masculine or acts in a masculine manner.

farce.definition: A style of humor marked by broad improbabilities with little regard to regularity or method.

slung.definition: To throw with a circular or arcing motion.

tipsy.definition: Slightly drunk, fuddled, staggering, foolish as a result of drinking alcoholic beverages

metal.definition: (heading) Chemical elements or alloys, and the mines where their ores come from.

yield.definition: To pay, give in payment; repay, recompense; reward; requite.

delve.definition: To dig the ground, especially with a shovel.

scour.definition: The removal of sediment caused by swiftly moving water.

gamer.definition: A person who plays any kind of game.

scrap.definition: A (small) piece; a fragment; a detached, incomplete portion.

money.definition: A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.

hinge.definition: A jointed or flexible device that allows the pivoting of a door etc.

album.definition: A book specially designed to keep photographs, stamps, or autographs.

asset.definition: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.

crept.definition: To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground.

creak.definition: The sound produced by anything that creaks; a creaking.

showy.definition: (sometimes derogatory) calling attention; flashy; standing out to the eye

depth.definition: The vertical distance below a surface; the degree to which something is deep

flood.definition: A (usually disastrous) overflow of water from a lake or other body of water due to excessive rainfall or other input of water.

trait.definition: An identifying characteristic, habit or trend.

payer.definition: One who pays; specifically, the person by whom a bill or note has been, or should be, paid.

goose.definition: Any of various grazing waterfowl of the family Anatidae, which have feathers and webbed feet and are capable of flying, swimming, and walking on land, and which are bigger than ducks.

float.definition: A buoyant device used to support something in water or another liquid.

atone.definition: To make reparation, compensation, amends or satisfaction for an offence, crime, mistake or deficiency.

primo.definition: The principal part of a duet.

apron.definition: An article of clothing worn over the front of the torso and/or legs for protection from spills; also historically worn by Freemasons and as part of women's fashion.

cacao.definition: A tree, Theobroma cacao, whose seed is used to make chocolate.

loser.definition: A person who loses; one who fails to win or thrive.

input.definition: The act or process of putting in; infusion.

gloat.definition: An act or instance of gloating.

brink.definition: The edge, margin, or border of a steep place, as of a precipice; a bank or edge.

beady.definition: Resembling beads; small, round, and gleaming.

droll.definition: A funny person; a buffoon, a wag.

lilac.definition: A large shrub of the genus Syringa, especially Syringa vulgaris, bearing white, pale-pink or purple flowers.

field.definition: A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; open country.

fluff.definition: Anything light, soft or fuzzy, especially fur, hair, feathers.

madam.definition: A polite form of address for a woman or lady.

bland.definition: Having a soothing effect; not irritating or stimulating.

augur.definition: A diviner who foretells events by the behaviour of birds or other animals, or by signs derived from celestial phenomena, or unusual occurrences.

flock.definition: A large number of birds, especially those gathered together for the purpose of migration.

elope.definition: (of a married person) To run away from home with a paramour.

cinch.definition: A simple saddle girth used in Mexico.

apart.definition: (Used after a noun or in the predicate) Exceptional, distinct.

scale.definition: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.

older.definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.

sperm.definition: The reproductive cell or gamete of the male; a spermatozoon.

abbot.definition: The superior or head of an abbey or monastery.

rerun.definition: A television program shown after its initial presentation — particularly many weeks after its initial presentation; a repeat.

quest.definition: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.

crisp.definition: A thin slice of fried potato eaten as a snack.

itchy.definition: Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.

cheer.definition: A cheerful attitude; happiness; a good, happy, or positive mood.

chard.definition: An edible leafy vegetable, Beta vulgaris subsp. cicla, with a slightly bitter taste.

leant.definition: To incline, deviate, or bend, from a vertical position; to be in a position thus inclining or deviating.

curse.definition: A supernatural detriment or hindrance; a bane.

swing.definition: The manner in which something is swung.

opine.definition: To have or express an opinion; to state as an opinion; to suppose, consider (that).

gumbo.definition: A soup or stew made with okra.

miner.definition: A person who works in a mine.

chasm.definition: A deep, steep-sided rift, gap or fissure; a gorge or abyss.

armor.definition: A protective layer over a body, vehicle, or other object intended to deflect or diffuse damaging forces.

mural.definition: A large painting, usually drawn on a wall.

legal.definition: The legal department of a company.

fatal.definition: A fatality; an event that leads to death.

moral.definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.

owing.definition: To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.

booth.definition: A small stall for the display and sale of goods.

elfin.definition: An elf; an inhabitant of fairy-land.

minim.definition: A half note, drawn as a semibreve with a stem.

decoy.definition: A person or object meant to lure somebody into danger.

tribe.definition: A socially, ethnically, or politically cohesive group of people.

birth.definition: The process of childbearing; the beginning of life.

odder.definition: Differing from what is usual, ordinary or expected.

ditch.definition: A trench; a long, shallow indentation, as for irrigation or drainage.

taffy.definition: A soft, chewy candy made from boiled molasses or brown sugar.

swoop.definition: An instance, or the act of suddenly plunging downward.

opera.definition: A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.

mirth.definition: The emotion usually following humour and accompanied by laughter; merriment; jollity; gaiety.

cabal.definition: # A putative, secret organization of individuals gathered for a political purpose.

bezel.definition: The sloping edge or face on a cutting tool.

chain.definition: A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.

nasal.definition: A medicine that operates through the nose; an errhine.

pubic.definition: Of, or relating to the area of the body adjacent to the pubis or the pubes.

raven.definition: Any of several, generally large and lustrous black species of birds in the genus Corvus, especially the common raven, Corvus corax.

plead.definition: To present (an argument or a plea), especially in a legal case.

gnash.definition: A sudden snapping of the teeth.

flaky.definition: Consisting of flakes or of small, loose masses; lying, or cleaving off, in flakes or layers; flakelike.

munch.definition: A location or restaurant where good eating can be expected.

dully.definition: In a dull manner; without liveliness; without lustre.

eking.definition: The act or process of adding.

thing.definition: That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.

shorn.definition: To cut, originally with a sword or other bladed weapon, now usually with shears, or as if using shears.

pygmy.definition: (often capitalized) A member of one of various Ancient Equatorial African tribal peoples, notable for their very short stature.

ranch.definition: A large plot of land used for raising cattle, sheep or other livestock.

wring.definition: A powerful squeezing or twisting action.

shore.definition: Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.

mamma.definition: The milk-secreting organ of female humans and other mammals which includes the mammary gland and the nipple or teat; a breast; an udder. (plural: mammae)

froze.definition: Especially of a liquid, to become solid due to low temperature.

style.definition: Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.

moose.definition: The largest member of the deer family (Alces americanus, sometimes included in Alces alces), of which the male has very large, palmate antlers.

antic.definition: A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.

drown.definition: To die from suffocation while immersed in water or other fluid.

vegan.definition: A person who does not eat, drink or otherwise consume any animal products

chess.definition: A board game for two players with each beginning with sixteen chess pieces moving according to fixed rules across a chessboard with the objective to checkmate the opposing king.

guppy.definition: A tiny freshwater fish, Poecilia reticulata, popular in home aquariums, that usually has a plain body and black or dark blue tail for the females and a more colorful tail for the males.

union.definition: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.

lever.definition: (except in generalized senses below) A crowbar.

lorry.definition: A motor vehicle for transporting goods, and in some cases people; a truck.

image.definition: An optical or other representation of a real object; a graphic; a picture.

cabby.definition: A cabdriver; someone who drives a taxi.

druid.definition: One of an order of priests among certain groups of Celts before the adoption of Abrahamic religions.

exact.definition: To demand and enforce the payment or performance of, sometimes in a forcible or imperious way.

truth.definition: True facts, genuine depiction or statements of reality.

spear.definition: A long stick with a sharp tip used as a weapon for throwing or thrusting, or anything used to make a thrusting motion.

chime.definition: A musical instrument producing a sound when struck, similar to a bell (e.g. a tubular metal bar) or actually a bell. Often used in the plural to refer to the set: the chimes.

stunk.definition: To have a strong bad smell.

batch.definition: The quantity of bread or other baked goods baked at one time.

gauge.definition: A measure; a standard of measure; an instrument to determine dimensions, distance, or capacity; a standard

rotor.definition: A rotating part of a mechanical device, for example in an electric motor, generator, alternator or pump.

crack.definition: A thin and usually jagged space opened in a previously solid material.

curve.definition: A gentle bend, such as in a road.

latte.definition: A drink of coffee made from espresso and steamed milk, generally topped with foam.

witch.definition: A person who practices witchcraft; a woman or man who practices witchcraft.

bunch.definition: A group of similar things, either growing together, or in a cluster or clump, usually fastened together.

repel.definition: To turn (someone) away from a privilege, right, job, etc.

anvil.definition: A heavy iron block used in the blacksmithing trade as a surface upon which metal can be struck and shaped.

meter.definition: (always meter) A device that measures things.

broth.definition: Water in which food (meat or vegetable etc) has been boiled.

madly.definition: In a mad manner; without reason or understanding; wildly.

dried.definition: Without water or moisture, said of something that has previously been wet or moist; resulting from the process of drying.

scene.definition: The location of an event that attracts attention.

known.definition: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

magma.definition: The molten matter within the earth, the source of the material of lava flows, dikes of eruptive rocks, etc.

roost.definition: The place where a bird sleeps (usually its nest or a branch).

punch.definition: A hit or strike with one's fist.

pasty.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A small item of clothing that conceals little more than the nipple of a woman's breast, primarily worn by female exotic dancers.

downy.definition: Having down, covered with a soft fuzzy coating as of small feathers or hair.

rapid.definition: (often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.

clang.definition: A loud, ringing sound, like that made by free-hanging metal objects striking each other.

anger.definition: A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.

drive.definition: Motivation to do or achieve something; ability coupled with ambition.

email.definition: A system for transferring messages from one computer to another, usually through a network.

music.definition: A series of sounds organized in time, employing melody, harmony, tempo etc. usually to convey a mood.

stuff.definition: Miscellaneous items or objects; (with possessive) personal effects.

bleep.definition: A brief high-pitched sound, as from some electronic device.

rider.definition: One who rides, often on a horse or a motorcycle.

folio.definition: A leaf of a book or manuscript

setup.definition: Equipment designed for a particular purpose; an apparatus.

verso.definition: The back side of a flat object which is to be examined visually, as for reading, such as a sheet, leaf, coin or medal;

fauna.definition: Animals considered as a group; especially those of a particular country, region, time.

gummy.definition: A sheep that is losing or has lost its teeth.

happy.definition: A happy event, thing, person, etc.

newly.definition: Very recently; in the immediate past.

fussy.definition: Anxious or particular about petty details.

relic.definition: That which remains; that which is left after loss or decay; a remaining portion.

guava.definition: A tropical tree or shrub of the myrtle family, Psidium guajava.

fudge.definition: A type of very sweet candy or confection, usually made from sugar, butter, and milk or cream. Often used in the US synonymously with chocolate fudge.

chirp.definition: A short, sharp or high note or noise, as of a bird or insect.

alibi.definition: The plea or mode of defense under which a person on trial for a crime proves or attempts to prove being in another place when the alleged act was committed

whine.definition: A long-drawn, high-pitched complaining cry or sound

petty.definition: (usually in the plural) A little schoolboy, either in grade or size.

plait.definition: A flat fold; a doubling, as of cloth; a pleat.

felon.definition: A person who has committed a felony.

gourd.definition: Any of the trailing or climbing vines producing fruit with a hard rind or shell, from the genera Lagenaria and Cucurbita (in Cucurbitaceae).

brown.definition: A colour like that of chocolate or coffee.

thrum.definition: A thrumming sound; a hum or vibration.

ficus.definition: A plant belonging to the genus Ficus, including the rubber plant.

stash.definition: A collection, sometimes hidden; a reserve.

wiser.definition: Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.

junta.definition: A council, convention, tribunal or assembly; especially, the grand council of state in Spain.

visor.definition: A part of a helmet, arranged so as to lift or open, and so show the face. The openings for seeing and breathing are generally in it.

scree.definition: Loose stony debris on a slope.

impel.definition: To urge a person; to press on; to incite to action or motion via intrinsic motivation.

press.definition: A device used to apply pressure to an item.

whose.definition: (interrogative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.

stoop.definition: The staircase and landing or porch leading to the entrance of a residence.

speak.definition: A low class bar, a speakeasy.

pulse.definition: A normally regular beat felt when arteries are depressed, caused by the pumping action of the heart.

staid.definition: Not capricious or impulsive; sedate, serious, sober.

hence.definition: From here, from this place, away

pinch.definition: The action of squeezing a small amount of a person's skin and flesh, making it hurt.

snore.definition: The act of snoring, and the noise produced.

ripen.definition: To grow ripe; to become mature (said of grain, fruit, flowers etc.)

snowy.definition: Marked by snow, characterized by snow.

attic.definition: The space, often unfinished and with sloped walls, directly below the roof in the uppermost part of a house or other building, generally used for storage or habitation.

leach.definition: A quantity of wood ashes, through which water passes, and thus imbibes the alkali.

mouth.definition: The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.

hound.definition: A dog, particularly a breed with a good sense of smell developed for hunting other animals.

clump.definition: A cluster or lump; an unshaped piece or mass.

tonal.definition: Of or relating to tones or tonality.

bigot.definition: One who is narrow-mindedly devoted to one's own ideas and groups, and intolerant of (people of) differing ideas, races, genders, religions, politics, etc.

peril.definition: A situation of serious and immediate danger.

piece.definition: A part of a larger whole, usually in such a form that it is able to be separated from other parts.

undid.definition: To reverse the effects of an action.

shine.definition: Brightness from a source of light.

gecko.definition: Any lizard of the family Gekkonidae. They are small, carnivorous, mostly nocturnal animals with large eyes and adhesive toes enabling them to climb on vertical and upside-down surfaces.

rodeo.definition: A gathering of cattle to be branded.

guard.definition: A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.

steer.definition: A suggestion about a course of action.

loamy.definition: Consisting of loam; partaking of the nature of loam; resembling loam.

scamp.definition: A rascal, swindler, or rogue; a ne'er-do-well.

scram.definition: Leave in a hurry, go away.

manly.definition: Having the characteristics of a man.

vaunt.definition: A boast; an instance of vaunting.

organ.definition: A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.

feral.definition: A domesticated animal that has returned to the wild; an animal, particularly a domesticated animal, living independently of humans.

knock.definition: Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.

extra.definition: Something additional, such as an item above and beyond the ordinary school curriculum, or added to the usual charge on a bill.

condo.definition: Joint sovereignty over a territory by two or more countries.

adapt.definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit

polka.definition: A lively dance originating in Bohemia.

skirt.definition: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.

faith.definition: A trust or confidence in the intentions or abilities of a person, object, or ideal from prior empirical evidence.

torso.definition: The main part of the (human) body that extends from the neck to the groin, excluding the head and limbs.

match.definition: A competitive sporting event such as a boxing meet, a baseball game, or a cricket match.

mercy.definition: Relenting; forbearance to cause or allow harm to another.

peace.definition: A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.

flush.definition: A group of birds that have suddenly started up from undergrowth, trees etc.

catty.definition: (of a person or remark) With subtle hostility in an effort to hurt, annoy or upset, particularly among women.

login.definition: A combination of a user's identification and password used to enter a computer, program, network, etc.

eject.definition: To compel (a person or persons) to leave.

roger.definition: (radio telecommunications) Received (used in radio communications to acknowledge that a message has been received and understood)

rival.definition: A competitor (person, team, company, etc.) with the same goal as another, or striving to attain the same thing. Defeating a rival may be a primary or necessary goal of a competitor.

untie.definition: To loosen, as something interlaced or knotted; to disengage the parts of.

refit.definition: The process of having something fitted again, repaired or restored.

aorta.definition: The great artery which carries the blood from the heart to all parts of the body except the lungs; the main trunk of the arterial system.

adult.definition: A fully grown human or animal.

judge.definition: A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.

artsy.definition: (sometimes derogatory) Inclined towards the arts; arty.

rural.definition: A person from the countryside; a rustic.

shave.definition: To make bald or shorter by using a tool such as a razor or pair of electric clippers to cut the hair close to the skin.
